mnemonics.title: Remember Row Space Basis
mnemonics.content: Row Space: REF gives non-zero rows - these form the basis as we go! (REF = Echelon Form)

mnemonics.title: Remember Column Space Basis
mnemonics.content: Column Space: Find RREF first, then pick the pivot columns from original matrix - pivot points to columns that matter!

mnemonics.title: Rank-Nullity Quick Check
mnemonics.content: Rank + Nullity = n (for A is m×n) - Just subtract rank from columns to find nullity!

acronyms.usage: Matrix form where leading entries move right as you go down rows
REF where each leading 1 is the only non-zero entry in its column
Same as RREF - use pivot columns to find basis for column space

visualTips.visualization: Imagine row space as a flat plane in n-dimensional space - all row combinations lie on this 'floor'

visualTips.concept: Column Space as Collection of Arrows
visualTips.visualization: Column vectors are like arrows starting from origin in m-dimensional space - their span creates a 'tube' or 'space'
Think of rank as how many independent directions the matrix can 'point' in - like degrees of freedom

analogies.analogy: Row space is like all possible linear equations you can derive from the original equations - they capture all information about the system.
Column space is like all possible output vectors you can create as linear combinations of input basis vectors.
Rank is like the 'useful information' in a matrix - just like how many truly independent pieces of data you have.
Null space represents 'hidden' solutions that don't affect output - like redundant information in equations.
